fills.note: Ruth pericope → the pinned Ruth BCD ref; canonical ref is the reviewer's call.

fills.value_json: {"_note":"Pericope stays INFORMAL_CASUAL (narrator's telling). MM Section 1 marks two scene-level shifts: S2 (4:14-15) the women's benediction lifts to CEREMONIAL; S3 (4:16-17) the lap/cradle settles to INTIMATE. No moment-level or framing overrides.","scene_level":[{"scene_id":"S2","override_value":"CEREMONIAL"},{"scene_id":"S3","override_value":"INTIMATE"}],"moment_level":null}
fills.note: Both overrides are explicitly marked in the MM's multi-level register tagging.

Closing pericope; existing arc tokens are pericope-specific and do not fit the marriage/gift/birth/restoration movement, so four mints + EMPTINESS_REVERSED. BLESSING_INVOCATION and NARRATOR_FRAMING_CLOSE reused.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM 2.1: 'Boaz takes Ruth, she becomes his wife, he comes to her' — the redemption completed in marriage; no existing arc token covers a consummated marriage.
MM 2.1/2.4: 'YHWH gives her conception' — the book's second direct divine act, a distinct arc beat with no approved equivalent.
MM 2.1: 'she bears a son' — the line's future delivered; no approved birth/heir arc token exists.
MM 2.1/2.4: 'the turn from death to a living name and from empty to full' — the deliberate reversal of the approved EMPTYING; needs its own token.
MM 2.4: 'closes the communal-naming the book opened in Naomi's bitter homecoming' — the women name the child for Naomi; no approved naming arc token.

fills.note: DIVINE_CONTEXT (YHWH gives conception; divine name returns), KINSHIP/INSTITUTIONAL (daughter-in-law, kinsman-redeemer), PRIOR_PERICOPE_CARRY_FORWARD + AUDIENCE_KNOWLEDGE (1:21, 2:20, 3:9, gate echoes), HISTORICAL_ERA (narrator's later David vantage).
ECONOMICAL = the bare swift verbs of v.13; WARM = the wide blessing; INTIMATE/STILLED = the lap image; RISING = the closing reach to David.
BRISK = swift opening line; SLOWED to the tender lap; SETTLES at the still image; WIDENS at the sudden genealogical reach.
CLOSES = resolves every thread / inclusio; ADVANCES = delivers the gift child; REACTIVATES = lands the redeemer-word and answers the gate-blessing; OPENS = onto the genealogy/David.
Boaz, the one who takes Ruth as wife.
Boaz named plainly; no marked reference.
Ruth, taken as wife and bearer of the son.
YHWH, giver of conception — the direct divine act.
The newborn son, the line's future.
'a son' (בֵּן); the deliberate not-yet-named status is carried by the B? being_id rather than a marked referential form.

Surface form 'a son' (בֵּן). The map deliberately gives no wikilink — the child is the as-yet-unnamed newborn, later named Obed (B25) in S3. Kept uncoded to preserve the 'not yet named' significant absence.
Considered MARRIAGE_SCENE but the scene's communicative heart is YHWH's gift of conception and the birth; minting BIRTH_SCENE. Reviewer may prefer MARRIAGE_SCENE.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 1 title 'The marriage, the gift, the birth' and 3F centers on the divine gift of conception and the birth of the son; no approved scene_kind covers a birth/gift resolution.

fills.note: The women of Bethlehem who bless Naomi. Minting TOWNSWOMEN rather than TOWNSPEOPLE because the gender is load-bearing — they are the female counterpart to the men/elders (FIG_0187).

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 2/3 'the women' / 'the neighbor-women' act as the female counterpart to the men's gate-blessing (FIG_0187); approved TOWNSPEOPLE is gender-blind and FEMALE_WORKERS is field-specific.

fills.note: Naomi is blessed here as the restored grandmother to whom the child is reckoned, not in her widow/MIL relation; minting GRANDMOTHER.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 2/3: 'the grandmother' to whom the child is reckoned (the once-empty widow now holding the line's future); no approved role captures the grandmother function central to this scene.

fills.note: YHWH, the one the women bless.
The newborn named by the women as 'a redeemer' (גֹּאֵל) YHWH has given Naomi.
Map explicitly flags the redeemer-word (גֹּאֵל) landing on the newborn — a marked naming. referential_form is the open descriptive axis (cf. gold UNNAMED_MAN_FROM_BETHLEHEM), so no mint declaration.
Surface form 'a redeemer' (גֹּאֵל); the same as-yet-unnamed newborn as S1, deliberately uncoded by the map until named Obed (B25) in S3.
Ruth, praised as the bearer, better than seven sons.
MM explicitly carries a 'Referential form:' field here — 'your daughter-in-law who loves you … better than seven sons'; the Moabite label is dropped and replaced by love. A map-flagged marked form.
The women's benediction over Naomi.
Naomi takes the child to her bosom and becomes his nurse; same GRANDMOTHER role as S2.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 3: the grandmother who takes the child to her bosom; reused from S2 (no approved equivalent).

fills.note: The child taken up and held, reckoned a son to Naomi.
'the child' (הַיֶּלֶד); plain reference, not marked.
Surface form 'the child' (הַיֶּלֶד); the held child kept uncoded, distinct from the named Obed (B25) entry — preserving the map's separation of the held child from the named heir.
The neighbor-women who call out the name and reckon the child to Naomi; same group as S2 (B24).

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 3 'the neighbor-women' (הַשְּׁכֵנוֹת) reused from S2; gender load-bearing for the communal-naming-for-Naomi figure (FIG_0181).

fills.note: 'the neighbor-women' epithet noted, but the prose does not call attention to a naming shift from S2; left unmarked to avoid over-marking.
Obed, the named child who heads the line to David — functions as the line's founding ancestor.
Jesse and David — the forward genealogical names the narrator reaches to; not ancestors of the scene but descendants/referents. Minting LINEAGE_REFERENT.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 3: Jesse and David are 'the names the narrator reaches to past the story' — forward genealogical referents; ANCESTOR is wrong-direction and ERA_REFERENT is time-setting only.

fills.note: David named without explanation (a significant absence), but this is the narrator's bare naming, not a marked epithet.

Considered NARRATOR_FRAMING_CLOSE_SCENE (this is the book's closing inclusio, FIG_0189), but the scene's dominant content is the lap and the naming; minting NAMING_SCENE. Reviewer may prefer NARRATOR_FRAMING_CLOSE_SCENE.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM Scene 3 title 'The child on Naomi's lap; the naming' and 3F center on the communal naming of the child; no approved scene_kind covers a naming event.

fills.note: Boaz takes Ruth as wife.
Narrated event (no top-level speech_act, per gold idiom). The take/become/come sequence kept compact at event-summary level.
The marriage leads into the conception (rapid agent shift, FIG_0180).
YHWH gives Ruth conception.
The book's second direct divine act (per MM 2.4).
Ruth bears a son. No approved proposition_kind covers childbirth; minting BORE.

vocabulary_additions.justification: MM P3 'bearing' — Ruth bears a son; no approved proposition_kind (DIED, GAVE, TOOK, etc.) covers a birth event.

fills.note: Child uncoded (the as-yet-unnamed newborn).
The women's benediction: blessed be YHWH.
'Blessed be YHWH' is doxological praise/declaration; STATES_AS_TRUE chosen as nearest closed speech act (no PRAISES/BLESSES exists). redeemer_referent is the newborn (uncoded).
The blessing responds to the birth.
Wish-portion of the benediction: may his name be called out.
A wish for the child (third party) — name-preservation (CB_0005).
The women declare the child's future role.
The women declare about Ruth — the ground of the blessing ('for your daughter-in-law … has borne him').
bearer_referential_form preserves the marked naming inside the proposition (cf. gold *_referential_form slots).
Naomi takes the child and sets him on her bosom.
Cheq as maternal-adoption locus (FIG_0185).
Narrator identifies Naomi's new role as the child's nurse (omenet). No BECAME kind exists; IDENTIFIED is the nearest fit for a narrated role-assignment.
Omenet as faithful sustainer (FIG_0186).
The neighbor-women call out a name, proclaiming a son born to Naomi.
The proclamation precedes the actual name (Obed) at P11; child still uncoded here.
They call his name Obed.
Child coded B25 here — the naming is the point at which the registry-named Obed appears.
The narrator's genealogical reach past the story to Jesse and David (FIG_0007, from a later time).
B26 covers both Jesse and David per registry; the four-step naming (FIG_0182) lands on David as the inclusio with the prologue.
Final proposition; the inclusio with the prologue (P01) is cross-pericope and carried by figure flags, not an in-pericope link.

No further decomposition undertaken. P1 (4:13a) carries three sub-events (take / become wife / come to her) but is kept at one summary proposition with compact slots, matching the MM's own single-proposition granularity and the gold idiom of holding swift verb-chains together. P12's two genealogical links are held in a components array inside its slots rather than split.

remarks: Heavy mint load is concentrated in this closing pericope because the approved enumerations are largely tuned to the earlier Ruth scenes; all mints are map-anchored and declared. The newborn is kept uncoded as B? in S1/S2 and in all slots through P10 to preserve the MM's deliberate 'not yet named' absence, switching to B25 only at the naming (P11–P12). referential_form values (REDEEMER_GOEL, DAUGHTER_IN_LAW_WHO_LOVES_YOU) follow the gold's open descriptive-token convention and are not declared as enumeration mints. Two scene_kind choices (BIRTH_SCENE for S1, NAMING_SCENE for S3) and the GRANDMOTHER/TOWNSWOMEN/LINEAGE_REFERENT role mints are the items most worth reviewer attention.
